docs.title: 🌊 Surf Weather API
docs.text: Build weather-powered applications using real-time coastal data.

docs.title: 🚀 Start Building
docs.text: The Surf Weather API provides developers with fast, reliable access to weather data for surf and coastal applications.

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text:
• 🌦 Retrieve real-time weather data
  • 📍 Query by city or location
  • 🔐 Secure API key authentication
  • ⚡ Integrate quickly using SDK examples

docs.title: 🧭 Developer Journey
docs.text:
Follow this path to get started quickly:

➡️ Getting Started ➡️ Authentication ➡️ First API Call ➡️ SDK Examples ➡️ Production Usage

docs.title: About This Project
docs.text: This documentation portal was created as a real-world simulation of startup API documentation.

docs.title: 🎯 Project Goal
docs.text:
The goal was to design a developer-first documentation system that demonstrates:

  • API documentation strategy
  • Developer onboarding flow
  • Versioned documentation architecture
  • Docs-as-code workflow

docs.title: 🧱 Tools Used
docs.text:
• MkDocs (Material Theme)
  • Markdown
  • OpenAPI Specification (YAML)
  • Git-based workflow

docs.title: 🧭 Documentation Strategy
docs.text:
The structure follows a developer journey:

  1. Introduction & onboarding
  2. Authentication
  3. First API call
  4. SDK usage
  5. Error handling
  6. Versioned API reference

This reduces friction for new developers.

docs.title: 🔄 Versioning Approach
docs.text:
- v1 → Stable production API
- v2 → Beta / future enhancements

Version folders allow independent evolution of APIs and documentation.

docs.title: 📦 API Reference Approach
docs.text:
Instead of embedding Swagger directly, the documentation links to the OpenAPI specification.

This mirrors many real-world startup practices where:

  • Guides remain lightweight
  • API testing is handled via Swagger Editor or dedicated portals.

docs.title: 💡 Key Skills Demonstrated
docs.text:
This project demonstrates:

  • API documentation planning
  • Information architecture
  • Developer experience thinking
  • Documentation lifecycle management
  • Docs-as-code workflows

docs.title: 🚀 Future Enhancements
docs.text:
Planned improvements include:

  • Automated API reference generation
  • Analytics-driven documentation improvements
  • Version comparison pages
  • SDK auto-generation

docs.title: 👨‍💻 Author
docs.text: Technical Documentation Portfolio Project Focused on Developer Experience and API Documentation.

docs.title: Documentation Architecture
docs.text: This developer portal is structured to simulate a real-world startup API documentation system.

docs.title: 🎯 Goals
docs.text:
The documentation is designed to:

  • Guide developers from onboarding to production usage
  • Separate conceptual guides from API reference material
  • Support versioned APIs (v1 stable, v2 beta)
  • Follow a docs-as-code workflow

docs.title: 🧭 Developer Journey Design
docs.text:
The navigation follows a logical developer workflow:

  1. Getting Started
  2. Authentication
  3. First API Call
  4. SDK Examples
  5. Error Handling

This structure reduces cognitive load for first-time users.

docs.title: 🧱 Versioning Strategy
docs.text:
API versions are separated into folders:

  • v1/ → Stable production API

  • v2/ → Beta / future development

This allows independent evolution of documentation and API lifecycle.

docs.title: 🔧 Docs-as-Code Approach
docs.text:
This project uses:

  • MkDocs
  • Markdown
  • Git-based workflow
  • OpenAPI specification (openapi.yaml)

Benefits:

  • Version control
  • Reviewable changes
  • Easy CI/CD integration
  • Scalable documentation architecture

docs.title: 📦 API Reference Strategy
docs.text:
Instead of embedding Swagger directly, the documentation links to the OpenAPI specification.

Reason:

  • Keeps documentation lightweight
  • Avoids plugin dependency issues
  • Matches many real-world documentation workflows

docs.title: 🚀 Future Improvements
docs.text:
Planned enhancements:

  • Automated API reference generation
  • Search analytics
  • Version comparison guides
  • SDK auto-generation

docs.title: 👨‍💻 Author Note
docs.text:
This documentation project demonstrates:

  • API lifecycle understanding
  • Developer experience thinking
  • Structured information architecture
  • Practical docs-as-code implementation

docs.text:
• Initial release
  • Current weather endpoint added

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All API requests require an API key.

docs.title: Authentication Method
docs.text:
Authentication is performed using query parameters.

Example: https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=Goa&appid=YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Never expose API keys publicly
  • Store keys in environment variables
  • Rotate keys periodically

docs.title: Common Errors
docs.text: Code Meaning 401 Invalid API key

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: ➡️ Continue to First API Call

docs.title: Error Catalog
docs.text:
This page lists common API errors and resolutions.

Code Description Resolution 400 Bad request Verify parameters 401 Unauthorized Check API key 404 Not found Verify endpoint 429 Rate limit exceeded Retry later 500 Server error Retry or contact support

docs.title: Debugging Tip
docs.text: Always log response status codes during development.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
Welcome to the Surf Weather API.

This guide helps you make your first successful API request.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin:

  • Create an OpenWeather account
  • Generate an API key
  • Install a REST client (Postman or curl)

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text: https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Never expose API keys publicly
  • Store keys in environment variables
  • Rotate keys periodically

docs.title: Common Errors
docs.text: Code Meaning 401 Invalid API key

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: ➡️ Continue to First API Call

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text: Rate limits prevent API abuse and ensure stability.

docs.title: Typical Limits
docs.text: Plan Requests Free Tier Limited requests per minute Paid Tier Higher request limits

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Cache responses where possible
  • Avoid polling too frequently
  • Implement retry logic

docs.title: Python SDK Example
docs.text: Example using Python requests library.

docs.title: Example Code
docs.text:
```python import requests

url = "https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather"

params = { "q": "Goa", "appid": "YOUR_API_KEY" }

response = requests.get(url, params=params) print(response.json())

docs.title: First API Call
docs.text: This tutorial walks you through your first request.

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=Goa&appid=YOUR_API_KEY

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text: ```json { "weather": [ { "description": "clear sky" } ] }

docs.title: Why is my location not showing?
docs.text: Try searching using a nearby city name or check spelling.

docs.title: Why are weather values delayed?
docs.text: Data refreshes every few minutes depending on provider updates.

docs.title: I cannot log in
docs.text:
• Verify email and password
  • Reset password using "Forgot Password"
  • Check internet connection

docs.title: Why is my location not showing?

docs.text: Weather data refreshes every few minutes.

  • Reset password from login page

docs.title: Contact Support
docs.text:
If you need help, contact:

support@surfweather.com

docs.title: Help Center
docs.text:
Welcome to the Help Center.

This section provides solutions to common issues and troubleshooting steps.

docs.title: Problem: Dashboard not loading
docs.text:
Possible causes

  • Slow internet connection
  • Temporary server delay

Solution

  1. Refresh the page.
  2. Clear browser cache.
  3. Try again after a few minutes.

docs.title: Problem: No weather data displayed
docs.text:
Possible causes - Invalid location - Data provider delay

Solution 1. Select another nearby location. 2. Check again after refresh.

docs.title: Problem: Alerts not working
docs.text:
Possible causes - Alerts disabled in settings - Notification permissions blocked

Solution

  1. Go to Settings → Alerts.
  2. Enable notifications.
  3. Allow browser notifications.

docs.title: Contact Support
docs.text:
If the issue continues, contact support:

📧 support@surfweather.com

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Welcome to Surf Weather.

This guide helps you start using the platform in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: Step 1 — Sign In
docs.text: Log in using your registered email and password.

docs.title: Step 2 — Select Location
docs.text: Use the search bar to find your coastal city or beach.

docs.title: Step 3 — View Surf Conditions
docs.text:
The dashboard displays:

  • Wave height
  • Wind speed
  • Temperature
  • Tide levels

docs.title: Step 4 — Save Favorite Locations
docs.text: Click ⭐ to save locations for quick access later.

docs.title: User Manual
docs.text: This manual explains all major features available to users.

docs.title: Dashboard Overview
docs.text: The dashboard provides real-time surf and weather insights.

docs.title: Key Sections
docs.text:
• Current Conditions
  • Hourly Forecast
  • Weekly Forecast
  • Alerts Panel

docs.title: Location Search
docs.text: Use the search bar to switch between locations.

docs.title: Alerts
docs.text:
Users can enable alerts for:

  • High waves
  • Strong winds
  • Storm warnings

docs.title: Account Settings
docs.text:
From the profile menu you can:

  • Change units (Celsius/Fahrenheit)
  • Manage saved locations
  • Update profile details
